posts.cooked:
Uhm, I searched through the forums, I found nothing about this so I decided to open a new thread.

Does anyone know where is all the info about projectiles stored in LBA2?
If so, would it be possible to swap them with other ones?
For example swapping pistolaser shots with rockets, or Funfrocks fireballs with Franco grenades
And with that I dont mean swapping their models, as I can do that easily

Oh, and who can explain me what in the world is ANIMS3D.HQR for? I tried swapping some entries but nothing seems to change.

I think ANIMS3D.HQR is for the high-def videos, not sure though.

And its impossible to swap projectiles, soz.

Dino-Fly:

I think ANIMS3D.HQR is for the high-def videos, not sure though.

And its impossible to swap projectiles, soz.

Aw, that sucks. It was something that I always wished to do.

I dont think ANIMS3D is for the videos, since the first two entries are labeled Twinsen walking and Twinsen running, yet I higly doubt it contains the animations used for character models, there already is ANIMS.HQR for that
But the projectile image is just a sprite, isnt it? It is in LBA 1, where I was able to replace the magic ball with a different image. (Though I couldnt change the physics.)

Yes.

That is correct for LBA 1.

As for LBA2, please remember that their ought to be redundancy (I assume) for all objects that exist in both the isometric and the real 3D view. They are stored differently. (I think)

Well I guess you can wait for someone more experienced to answer, who doesnt just speculate
I dont think that the magic ball outside is three-dimensional, you cant really see it in different lighting/from different angles than inside.
Some of the projectiles are 3D (lasers, darts probably), and others are 2D (magic ball, blowgun balls). The trajectories and damage are probably hard-coded in the game, so no change is possible.

I know I can change their appearance but replacing the darts with the groboclone rockets is good for a laugh and nothing more, since they still act like darts.
Yeah, I was afraid that they were hard coded. Oh well.

In LBA1 and 2 there are a lot of common things split in various files.

The Projectiles information in LBA (1&2) are stored under Animation Actions. This actions are in different places from both games. In LBA1 it is on FILE3D.HQR and in LBA2 is only store at 1 single entry at RESS.HQR (#45)

The animations tells the engine it have a projectile of a type to be throw in a certain keyframe with a 3D object or sprite. You can swap sprites or 3D objects from projectiles but it will make the entire game to use that change. So, if you want to make a change in only 1 animation you will need to change those entries above.

Sorry to be a thread necromancer (on my own thread nonetheless) , but Im having my annual urge to play/screw with LBA2 and yep, you guessed it. Im STILL trying to figure out how to edit projectiles

So, I get that projectile information is stored on entry 45 of RESS.hqr
Is there any way a mere mortal with no hacking skills can edit this information?

Alexfonts answer seemed to imply that it was possible, but after messing around with programs like LBA pack editor and the likes I got nothing. If anyone is able and willing to clarify that for me it would be much appreciated!

From what I understand the animation contains information what projectile to fire and at what frame. So if you want to change a projectile for an animation to another one, you will rather have to edit the animations, not projectiles themselves. As far as I know we dont have any editor for LBA 2 animations, so you would have to hex-edit them, and I dont know if we have the file format description anywhere.

yes, we dont have yet that format, but its almost identical to LBA1 file. There are some programs, the ones that shows LBA2 objects, that have partially loaded this file. I will try to grab some more info for you.
That would be super. Much appreciated.
The actual projectile sprites are in SPRITES.HQR and SPRIRAW.HQR.
Im not interested in the sprites as much as the projectiles themselves. I know I can make a dart look like a rocket, but it wont explode on impact like one. Thats what Id like to do.
- Lawrence: That information is in FILE3D.HQR.
